decisions.decision: Keep Torch native LayerNorm forward as reference/operator evidence only. It does not produce the saved mean/rstd state required by the GPT-2 trainer backward contract; stats-compatible Torch compositions are much slower than CUDA.
evidence: 2026-05-22 optional refresh scratch/sm120_rounds/codex_sm120_current_optional_refresh_20260522: Torch native LayerNorm forward C=768 measured 139.712 us versus CUDA 140.567 us, a 0.608% operator-only edge.
The trainer LayerNorm forward contract stores mean and rstd for backward; the Torch native forward row has no saved mean/rstd.
2026-05-22 optional refresh stats-compatible Torch row Torch LayerNorm ForwardWithStats C=768 measured 2183.296 us, far slower than the CUDA trainer row.
